mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals imminent corporate earnings declines, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in 70 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 6.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand growth, which forces equity valuations higher over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day SPX returns with a Rank IC exceeding 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and output, which directly translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days during expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases produce negative or flat 20-day SPX returns during late-cycle expansion periods, with a t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive CPIAUCSL month-over-month changes correlate with positive 5-day SPX returns, yielding a Rank IC greater than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll employment drop exceeding 500 thousand signals severe economic contraction, forcing equity markets to price in recessionary earnings cuts over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines by more than 500 thousand in a single month
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns remain positive in over 65 percent of months where PAYEMS falls by more than 500 thousand.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based economic demand strength, which sustains corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 and RSAFS shows no predictive power for 20-day SPX direction, with out-of-sample accuracy below 52 percent.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation remains elevated above 255, the Federal Reserve maintains restrictive policy stances that suppress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in 70 percent of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 255 and FEDFUNDS remains unchanged.
